_comment: We do NOT read the matched button's own rendered background: primary/glow
buttons paint their themed surface via <BorderGlow> on a CANVAS (and hover via
::before), so the element's own computed background is a flat fallback. Instead
we read TOKEN values: for each probe spec we append a probe child to every
matched element, apply `token` to the real CSS property `apply`, and read back
the `read` longhands. Custom properties inherit, so the probe resolves the
button's EFFECTIVE token (theme baseline + any feature/element override).
Result per archetype × theme: ONE value tuple = the token value to write into
:root.theme-X { --…: }. MORE THAN ONE = drift (a feature override sets the token
differently on some page) → reconcile or promote to a real variant.
Selectors target the rendered signal: <Button> emits data-variant + literal
class 'button' (Button.jsx:81-83), so [data-variant] is the canonical marker.
